op.name: isreal
op.notes: Test if element is real (imag == 0) element-wise.
op.summary: Returns a bool array, where True if input element is real.

blocks.text: Returns a bool array, where True if input element is real.

If element has complex type with zero imaginary part, the return value
for that element is True.

desc_blocks.text: Boolean array of same shape as `x`.

blocks.text: `isreal` may behave unexpectedly for string or object arrays (see examples)

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> a = np.array([1+1j, 1+0j, 4.5, 3, 2, 2j], dtype=complex)
>>> np.isreal(a)
array([False,  True,  True,  True,  True, False])

blocks.text: The function does not work on string arrays.

blocks.code:
>>> a = np.array([2j, "a"], dtype="U")
>>> np.isreal(a)  # Warns about non-elementwise comparison
False

blocks.text:
Returns True for all elements in input array of ``dtype=object`` even if
any of the elements is complex.

blocks.code:
>>> a = np.array([1, "2", 3+4j], dtype=object)
>>> np.isreal(a)
array([ True,  True,  True])

blocks.text: isreal should not be used with object arrays

blocks.code:
>>> a = np.array([1+2j, 2+1j], dtype=object)
>>> np.isreal(a)
array([ True,  True])

blocks.text: isrealobj — Return True if x is not a complex type.
